articles.title: My Adventures with Green Lantern makes Jessica Cruz a magical girl
articles.excerpt: Green Lantern is a Sailor Moon-style magical girl in DC's new animated series My Adventures with Green Lantern.
articles.body: Adult Swim's My Adventures with Superman redefined the Man of Steel by fusing comic book action with shonen anime sensibilities. Now the series is reaching for another classic genre for My Adventures with Green Lantern , which turns Green Lantern Jessica Cruz into a magical girl.
articles.author: Sam Nelson

articles.title: How to unlock every planet and route in Star Fox
articles.excerpt: Every planet in Star Fox has an alternate route, for a total of 25 different paths. Here's every planet in Star Fox and how to unlock it.
articles.body: The number of planets in Star Fox might look paltry, but unlocking every planet and route is rather more complex than it initially seems. Star Fox , like Star Fox 64 before it, has 14 planets. Each campaign includes seven missions, though your run always starts with Corneria and ends with a variation of Venom. That leaves five missions in between, and which planets you end up on will depend on what you accomplish during the stage.
articles.author: Josh Broadwell

articles.title: The U.S. Strongarms Polestar Out Of The American EV Market
articles.excerpt: The Swedish automaker, owned by the Chinese parent Geely, will not be allowed to sell EVs in the U.S. model year 2027 onward.
articles.body: Polestar, the Swedish automaker known for its electric vehicles, faces a major challenge in the American market. A recent development indicates a significant shift in the landscape for EV manufacturers. This situation highlights the complex interplay between global automotive brands
articles.author: InsideEVs

articles.title: We built a bidirectional context loop between web apps and AI agents
articles.excerpt: Source code: Tabforge AI You send a prompt → the model returns an answer → you try to glue it into your app. It works, but it always feels slightly disconnected from what’s actually happening in the U…
articles.body: Source code: Tabforge AI Most AI integrations in business apps feel the same: You send a prompt → the model returns an answer → you try to glue it into your app. It works, but it always feels slightly disconnected from what’s actually happening in the UI. The AI doesn’t really know what the user is doing. And the app doesn’t really know what the AI just did. We ran into that problem while building EasyAI / TabForge, and ended up with something we didn’t originally set out to build: a bidirectional context loop between the application and the AI runtime.
articles.author: Svetozar Radojcin

articles.title: AI agents are becoming real economic actors. They deserve a chain built for them, not borrowed from us.
articles.excerpt: The next major blockchains will be built around AI, not with AI bolted on afterward. That's the bet behind NOVAI. Right now, when an AI agent needs to transact, it reaches for crypto, because crypto i…
articles.body: The next major blockchains will be built around AI, not with AI bolted on afterward. That's the bet behind NOVAI. Right now, when an AI agent needs to transact, it reaches for crypto, because crypto is the closest available fit: permissionless, programmatic, no human in the loop gating a payment. But "closest available fit" isn't "built for the purpose." Existing chains were designed for humans, and agents are squatting on rails that were never meant for them. As agents take on more meaningful economic weight, that mismatch only gets more expensive. NOVAI is a from-scratch layer-1, written in Rust, where AI agents are first-class protocol primitives, not smart contracts running on a human chain.
articles.author: NOVAInetwork

articles.title: Do Developers Ever Really Disconnect?
articles.excerpt: Every year, around the time I'm about to take a few days off, the same questions come up. Should I bring the laptop? What if I get a good idea for that side project? Is it bad to push a small commit w…
articles.body: Every year, around the time I'm about to take a few days off, the same questions come up. Should I bring the laptop? What if I get a good idea for that side project? Is it bad to push a small commit while I'm at the beach? These aren't just personal anxieties. They reflect something specific about being a software developer. Our work and our curiosity often live in the same place, and it's hard to know where one ends and the other begins. The problem with "just checking in" There's research on this. A UGA meta-analysis of 32 studies from nine countries found that employees who psychologically disengaged from work during vacation saw the most improvement in their well-being.
articles.author: Ender Ahmet Yurt

articles.title: MCP Connection Issues: Why My MCP Server Kept Dropping Connections and How I Fixed It (After 86 Production Outages)
articles.excerpt: MCP Connection Issues: Why My MCP Server Kept Dropping Connections and How I Fixed It (After 86 Production Outages) Honestly, I thought I had everything figured out after 86 outages. Logging was done.…
articles.body: MCP Connection Issues: Why My MCP Server Kept Dropping Connections and How I Fixed It (After 86 Production Outages) Honestly, I thought I had everything figured out after 86 outages. Logging was done. Health checks were in place. Tool discovery had fuzzy matching. Authentication handled every possible location. Rate limiting was working. But my MCP server kept dropping connections randomly. Three days of debugging later, I found the problem — and it wasn't what I expected. It was all about SSE (Server-Sent Events) streaming. If you're building an MCP server, you need to read this. I saved you three days of headache. The Problem: Random Connection Drops Let me set the scene. My MCP server has been running in production for a while. Everything works fine most of the time.
articles.author: KevinTen

articles.title: Every important change Critical Role made in The Legend of Vox Machina season 4 compared to Campaign 1
articles.excerpt: Explore the significant changes made in The Legend of Vox Machina season 4 compared to the original Critical Role campaign.
articles.body: Critical Role has never been afraid to change the events of their popular live-play Dungeons & Dragons show while adapting it for TV. That was already obvious after the first three seasons of their Prime Video animated series, The Legend of Vox Machina , and it's only become more true in season 4, which just aired its final episode this week. (Don't worry, season 5 is already confirmed.)

articles.title: AI Won’t Just Build the Next App. It Will Rebuild the Old Ones.
articles.excerpt: The biggest AI opportunity in web development might not be the next shiny app. It might be the old one your company is afraid to touch. Every day, businesses run on software that still works but no lo…
articles.body: The biggest AI opportunity in web development might not be the next shiny app. It might be the old one your company is afraid to touch. Every day, businesses run on software that still works but no longer feels modern. Old admin panels. Internal CRMs. Laravel dashboards built years ago. Blade views nobody wants to refactor. Reporting tools with confusing filters. Support screens with too many columns. Customer portals that make money but slow down every new feature. That is where things get interesting. Most AI conversations focus on building something new from scratch. A founder has an idea, generates a prototype, writes some code with an AI assistant, connects a model API, and launches an MVP. That is a real use case, and it matters.
articles.author: VitaliiK

articles.title: XGIMI’s laser projector trio brings Google TV and a price tag to match
articles.excerpt: XGIMI's new Elfin Flip Laser, Flip 4K, and MIRA UST offer more clarity, but aren't cheap.
articles.body: Credit: XGIMI TL;DR After teasing new laser projectors earlier this month, XGIMI is now revealing the remaining key details, including the price. The Elfin Flip series gets two laser projectors, one with a 1080p resolution and another with 4K. They’re accompanied by an ultra-short-throw laser projector, MIRA, with Dolby Vision support. Earlier this month, noted projector brand XGIMI teased three new laser projectors headed for a global launch. Today, it has revealed more details on these devices, including their prices, and opened up preorders in Europe and the US. The launches today include the Elfin Flip Laser, Elfin Flip 4K, and MIRA UST. The first two add to the existing Elfin series of projectors, which rotate along an axis at the middle of the stand.

articles.title: Star Fox takes 2 hours to beat, but much longer long to master
articles.excerpt: Even with new cutscenes, Star Fox's story mode is over in under two hours. Here's what to do after the credits roll.
articles.body: If you’ve never played Star Fox 64 before, you’re in for a treat with its glitzy new Nintendo Switch 2 remake. Star Fox gives the classic shoot-’em-up a cinematic makeover that makes it feel like you’re playing a Nintendofied Top Gun . Just be prepared for a shock if you’re used to playing modern games padded with content: the main Campaign can be cleared in under two hours. That’s how it was on the Nintendo 64, and it doesn’t change much with the addition of new cutscenes.

articles.title: Another 'Star' is born: SpaceX names AI megaconstellation 'Starmind'
articles.excerpt: SpaceX will call its planned AI megaconstellation "Starmind," continuing the company's stellar naming theme.
articles.body: Once again, SpaceX has looked to the stars for naming inspiration. Elon Musk confirmed on Tuesday (June 23) that SpaceX will call its planned AI satellite megaconstellation " Starmind ". The choice should come as no surprise, as it continues the company's long-running stellar naming theme. Here's a rundown: Starship : SpaceX's next-generation launch vehicle, the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built. Starship is designed to be fully and rapidly reusable, a breakthrough that Musk thinks will make Mars settlement economically feasible Starbase : The company's facility in South Texas, which serves as Starship's manufacturing and launch hub. In May 2025, Starbase became an incorporated city . Starlink : SpaceX's broadband network in low Earth orbit .
articles.author: Mike Wall

articles.title: Traveling to see the total solar eclipse this August? This solar binocular Prime Day deal is a total-ly bright idea!
articles.excerpt: These Celestron EclipSmart 12x50 solar binoculars are perfect for watching the total solar eclipse safely this August 12, but you can use them the rest of the year, too.
articles.body: Who doesn't love a good total solar eclipse , now that we know they're not a portent of the world ending? The next one is on August 12 and these Celestron EclipSmart 12x50 solar binoculars are just what you need to observe the sun safely . Get these Celestron EclipSmart 12x50 solar binoculars right now at Amazon for $100 . Our expert, Jamie Carter, awarded them four stars in his Celestron EclipSmart 12x50 solar binoculars review , praising their image quality and ease of use. But if you buy them, don't put them away post-eclipse. I've become addicted to sungazing, zooming into our sun and observing its shifting sunspots . It's a fascinating glimpse into how our star works and the turmoil going on beneath its visible surface. Pick these binoculars up and you could be as hooked as I am.

articles.body: Insomniac Games has responded to a fan on social media who asked if the physical version of Marvel's Wolverine is just a code in the box — with the question now top of mind following Rockstar’s shock decision not to sell GTA 6 on disc at launch. Yesterday, Rockstar confirmed the physical version of GTA 6 comes with a download code inside the box . There is no disc. This has proven to be a huge talking point, with some fans even saying they won’t buy the game because of it. Video game preservation enthusiasts have criticised Rockstar , and some retailers have even refused to sell the game because of it . One follow-up question is, has Rockstar just hit the final nail in the coffin for the video game disc?

articles.title: What time is it on the moon? The US and China disagree
articles.excerpt: Why making lunar standard time is in everyone's best interests.
articles.body: If you stand outside the old Corn Exchange in Bristol, you'll see a clock with two minute hands above the entrance. One hand is set to London time, the other to Bristol's — ten minutes behind. The lag is because the sun reaches its peak over the second city a little bit after the first. Of course, when it comes to scheduling anything with bounds beyond one city, having two poses an issue. This is why, in 1840, the British company Great Western Railway imposed what it called "Railway Time" across its whole network of trains, establishing Greenwich Mean Time as the first standardized time. And it's still the time zone used in the U.K. today.
articles.author: Tom Brown

articles.title: OpenAI is making GPT-5.5 Instant ‘more fun’ to talk to
articles.excerpt: ChatGPT is getting more tasteful and refined with its responses.
articles.body: TL;DR OpenAI has made GPT-5.5 Instant more fun to talk to. It’s now better at handling multiple requirements and constraints, understanding the goal behind the question, and answering in a more conversational manner. The update is rolling out to paid users already, and free users will get it within a day. OpenAI has made several improvements to GPT-5.5 since it first announced the model back in April. However, while most of those were related to better, more accurate responses , the company has now pushed a change that’s less about math-solving prowess and more about conversational capabilities.
articles.author: Akshay Gangwar
